description_long: This object is a cylindrical structure composed of large, uniformly cut stone blocks arranged in horizontal rows. The stones have a slightly rough, matte surface with visible seams and subtle irregularities, giving a hand-crafted appearance. The cylinder is hollow, with an open top and a continuous, unbroken wall, suggesting it could serve as a well, planter, or barrier. The coloration is a consistent earthy tan, typical of sandstone or weathered limestone. The block joints are tightly fitted, with no visible mortar, and the overall form is robust and solid. There are no visible engravings, markings, or significant weathering patterns on the surface.
description: A cylindrical, hollow structure made of tightly fitted tan stone blocks with a rough, matte finish and no visible markings.
description_view_0: Front view shows the cylindrical shape and the clear horizontal rows of stone blocks with visible seams.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the uniformity of the stone blocks and the continuous, unbroken wall.
description_view_2: Another side view, similar to view 1, with the same block pattern and open top visible.
description_view_3: This view mirrors the previous side, emphasizing the consistent block arrangement and surface texture.
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no moss, chips, or erosion.
